Voice Inflection
To demonstrate what you can do with your voice, I’m going to take one sentence, which has eight words
in it, and make those eight words mean eight different things. 1)I did not say he stole the money. (Simple
fact statement.) 2) I did not say that he stole the money. (Implies that it was someone else who said it.)
3) I DID NOT say he stole the money. (This is vigorous denial that you said it.) 4) I did not SAAAYY he stole
the money. (Hints that you may have implied it but you didn’t say it.) 5) I did not say HE stole the money.
(Implies that someone other than the accused stole the money.) 6) I did not say he STOLE the money.
(You hint he might have borrowed it.) 7) I did not say he stole THE money. (He might have stolen some
money but not THE money.) 8) I did not say he stole the MONEY. (suggests that he might have stolen
something but certainly  not the money.)
***These are the same words, but with an educated change in your voice inflection, you can change the
meaning.
www.getwsodownload.com

CHANGE
*Most people do not plan to fail; they simply fail to plan. This program will help you build a blueprint for your
whole lifestyle that you can follow far into your future.
*It’s a funny thing about life; if you refuse to accept anything but the best, you very often get it.
*Many receive advice; only the wise profit from it.
*You will become as small as your controlling desire, as great as your dominant aspiration.
*Belief in himself is one of a man’s most difficult accomplishments.
*Most people spend more time planning a two-week vacation than they do planning their lives. They spend more
time mapping out the honeymoon than they do the marriage.
*If you fail to plan, you plan to fail.
*Success comes in “cans”- failure comes in “cant’s”
*Every triumph of the human spirit begins with a single step taken in faith.
*There are no born winners. There are no born losers. There are only born choosers.
*You can make money or you can make excuses. You can’t make both.
*It’s time to stop thinking about achievement and start doing something about it.
*We’re in the age of opportunity. Never has the opportunity for succeeding been greater.
*If you want to become financially independent, you have to learn and apply the rules that create financial
independence.
*People who are cautious in the extreme, who are afraid to take risks even when the odds are in their favor tend to
be afraid of life itself. Such persons are not likely to succeed in the business or anything else.
*Risks are a part of life. So is an occasional failure. So is success.
*”Things may come to those who wait, but only things left by those who hustle.” –Abraham Lincoln
*Yesterday is a cancelled check; tomorrow is a promissory note; today is the only cash you have, so spend wisely.”
*Failures are divided into two classes-those who thought and never did, and whose who did and never thought.
*He who believes is strong; he who doubts is weak. Strong Convictions proceed great actions.
*Recognizing  a problem doesn’t always bring a solution, but until we recognize that problem, there can be no
solution.
*Only a small number of people will actually be financially independent in the future. The rest do plan to fail, they
fail to plan.
*Motivation means dissatisfaction with the status quo.
*Lasting change only comes when the need for change is both understood and internalized.
www.getwsodownload.com
